events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: HVAC-R brazing & evacuation work, rough-complete stage. Observed condition: vacuum not held at target microns (decay test - read the gauge, FD). This means the evacuation didn't pass the decay test - the system isn't holding vacuum at the required micron level, indicating leaks or moisture/non-condensables still present.

This is non-compliant because the system must hold vacuum at target microns per manufacturer specs and industry standards. The work is rough-complete but the evacuation failed.

Decision: fail
Risk: medium-high (system could have leaks/moisture leading to compressor failure)
Component conditions: non-compliant, installed-defective (the brazing joints may be leaking)
Percent complete: maybe 70-80% roughed in but evacuation failed
Value: could be the micron reading but we don't have a specific number, just "not held at target"

Let me construct the JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant", "installed-defective"],
  "percent_complete": 75,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Evacuation decay test failed — system does not hold vacuum at target microns, indicating residual moisture, non-condensables, or a leak at a brazed joint",
    "Brazed connections may have pinhole leaks or incomplete penetration not detectable until vacuum isolation test"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Pressurize system with dry nitrogen and leak-test all brazed joints with electronic leak detector or soap solution",
    "Re-brazing any leaking joints after nitrogen purge",
    "Recover refrigerant if any was introduced, then re-evacuate using a two-stage vacuum pump with core tools/deep vacuum rig",
    "Repeat micron decay test to confirm system holds at or below manufacturer-specified target (commonly 500 microns or less) with isolation valve closed"
  ],
  "rationale": "A failed vacuum decay test at rough-complete stage means the brazing and evacuation work does not meet manufacturer or ACCA/EPA requirements. The system cannot be charged and placed in service until it holds the target micron level, confirming leak-free brazed joints and adequate dehydration.",
  "references": [
    "mfr specs — evacuation target microns and decay test acceptance criteria",
    "ACCA — system installation/evacuation best practices",
    "EPA 608 — refrigerant handling and system integrity requirements"
  ]
}
